title: Authors and Citation
text: Tadhg Moore. Author, maintainer. Chris McBride. Author.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Moore T, McBride C (2026). ltapi: Fetch data LimnoTrack API web services. R package version 0.0.0.9000, https://limnotrack.com/ltapi/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {ltapi: Fetch data from LimnoTrack API and web services},   author = {Tadhg Moore and Chris McBride},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://limnotrack.com/ltapi/}, }

what: ltapi - A package for accessing the LimnoTrack API
title: ltapi - A package for accessing the LimnoTrack API
text: goal ltapi provide simple, consistent interface querying accessing lake water quality data LimnoTrack database directly R. wraps LimnoTrack PostgREST API, handling authentication, error handling, response parsing can focus analysis.

title: ltapi - A package for accessing the LimnoTrack API
text: can install development version ltapi GitHub :

title: ltapi - A package for accessing the LimnoTrack API
text: typical workflow starts exploring tables available, inspecting table’s schema, fetching data optional filters.

title: ltapi - A package for accessing the LimnoTrack API
code: library(ltapi)  # List all available tables in the LimnoTrack database lt_tables() #>  [1] "aeme_output"           "calibration_metadata"  "depth_contours"        #>  [4] "forecast_ensembles"    "forecast_models"       "forecast_variables"    #>  [7] "function_metadata"     "geography_columns"     "geometry_columns"      #> [10] "lake_contours"         "lake_depth_points"     "lake_metadata"         #> [13] "lake_shape_full_84"    "lake_shape_light"      "lake_shape_light_84"   #> [16] "lernzmp_catchments"    "lernzmp_dem"           "lernzmp_lakes"         #> [19] "lernzmp_lcdb"          "lernzmp_reaches"       "lernzmp_subcatchments" #> [22] "met_data"              "parameter_metadata"    "raster_columns"        #> [25] "raster_overviews"      "sensitivity_metadata"  "simulation_metadata"   #> [28] "site_parameters"       "sites"                 "spatial_ref_sys"       #> [31] "system_log"            "water_profile"         "weather_forecast"

title: ltapi - A package for accessing the LimnoTrack API
text: fetching data can useful check available columns, types, descriptions:
code: lt_schema("lernzmp_lakes") #> # A tibble: 15 × 4 #>    column     type    format                             description #>    <chr>      <chr>   <chr>                              <chr>       #>  1 id         integer integer                            <NA>        #>  2 id_final   string  text                               <NA>        #>  3 lernzmp_id string  text                               <NA>        #>  4 name_final string  text                               <NA>        #>  5 region     string  text                               <NA>        #>  6 gm_type    string  text                               <NA>        #>  7 depth_meas boolean boolean                            <NA>        #>  8 elevation  number  double precision                   <NA>        #>  9 z_max      number  double precision                   <NA>        #> 10 z_mean     number  double precision                   <NA>        #> 11 dv         number  double precision                   <NA>        #> 12 v          number  double precision                   <NA>        #> 13 source     string  text                               <NA>        #> 14 area_ha    number  double precision                   <NA>        #> 15 geom       string  public.geometry(MultiPolygon,2193) <NA>

title: ltapi - A package for accessing the LimnoTrack API
text: Use lt_fetch() lt_filter() query specific lake:
code: wq <- lt_fetch(   table  = "water_profile",   filter = lt_filter(site_id == "Rotorua"),   order  = "datetime.asc",   limit = 1000 )  wq #> # A tibble: 1,000 × 6 #>    site_id datetime            parameter   value qc_level qc_comment #>    <chr>   <dttm>              <chr>       <dbl>    <int> <chr>      #>  1 Rotorua 2026-03-09 21:04:16 Cond      206.         400 ""         #>  2 Rotorua 2026-03-09 21:04:16 DOconc     NA          100 ""         #>  3 Rotorua 2026-03-09 21:04:16 DOconc      8.39       400 ""         #>  4 Rotorua 2026-03-09 21:04:16 DOpsat     92.5        400 ""         #>  5 Rotorua 2026-03-09 21:04:16 DptSns      1          400 ""         #>  6 Rotorua 2026-03-09 21:04:16 FlChlr      4.88       400 ""         #>  7 Rotorua 2026-03-09 21:04:16 FlPhyc      3.45       400 ""         #>  8 Rotorua 2026-03-09 21:04:16 ORP       379.         400 ""         #>  9 Rotorua 2026-03-09 21:04:16 PCchla      0.706      400 ""         #> 10 Rotorua 2026-03-09 21:04:16 pH          7.27       400 ""         #> # ℹ 990 more rows

title: ltapi - A package for accessing the LimnoTrack API
text: Tables geometry columns automatically returned sf objects:
code: contours <- lt_fetch(   table  = "lake_contours",   filter = lt_filter(lernzmp_id == "LID40188") )  class(contours) #> [1] "sf"         "data.frame"

title: ltapi - A package for accessing the LimnoTrack API
code: library(ggplot2) #> Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 4.5.3  ggplot(wq, aes(x = datetime, y = value, colour = factor(qc_level))) +   geom_point() +   facet_wrap(~parameter) +   labs(     title = "Lake Rotorua Data",     x     = NULL,     y     = "Value",     colour = "QC Level"   ) +   scale_x_datetime(date_labels = "%Y-%m-%d") +   theme_bw() #> Warning: Removed 125 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`).

title: ltapi - A package for accessing the LimnoTrack API
text: lt_filter() supports standard R comparison operators translates PostgREST query expressions:
code: # Single condition lt_fetch("lernzmp_lakes", filter = lt_filter(z_max >= 5), limit = 5) #> Simple feature collection with 5 features and 14 fields #> Geometry type: MULTIPOLYGON #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: 1755087 ymin: 5414930 xmax: 1795036 ymax: 5440216 #> Projected CRS: NZGD2000 / New Zealand Transverse Mercator 2000 #>       z_max  source        v id depth_meas     region    area_ha elevation #> 1  8.719000 LERNZmp 28317807  1       TRUE Wellington 651.795952  1.888864 #> 2 14.428886    <NA>       NA  2       TRUE Wellington  10.820019        NA #> 3 14.804648    <NA>       NA  3       TRUE Wellington  21.311029        NA #> 4  5.329407    <NA>       NA  7      FALSE Wellington   9.166009        NA #> 5  9.622161    <NA>       NA  9       TRUE Wellington  10.586849        NA #>   id_final   gm_type      name_final      dv   z_mean lernzmp_id #> 1    LID 1 Shoreline           Onoke 1.49103 4.333430       LID1 #> 2    LID 3 Shoreline Kohangapiripiri      NA 8.452708       LID3 #> 3    LID 4 Shoreline     Kohangatera      NA 9.029223       LID4 #> 4  LID 299  Riverine            <NA>      NA 1.995162     LID299 #> 5  LID 301  Riverine            <NA>      NA 3.034766     LID301 #>                         geometry #> 1 MULTIPOLYGON (((1779244 541... #> 2 MULTIPOLYGON (((1755299 541... #> 3 MULTIPOLYGON (((1755868 541... #> 4 MULTIPOLYGON (((1794634 543... #> 5 MULTIPOLYGON (((1794257 543...  # Multiple conditions lt_fetch("lernzmp_lakes", filter = lt_filter(   region == "Bay of Plenty",   z_max    >= 50 )) #> Simple feature collection with 6 features and 14 fields #> Geometry type: MULTIPOLYGON #> Dimension:     XY #> Bounding box:  xmin: 1892203 ymin: 5757318 xmax: 1935670 ymax: 5787533 #> Projected CRS: NZGD2000 / New Zealand Transverse Mercator 2000 #>     z_max  source          v   id depth_meas        region   area_ha elevation #> 1  89.134 LERNZmp  484880049  633       TRUE Bay of Plenty 1047.4205  318.4144 #> 2  52.100 LERNZmp         NA  639       TRUE Bay of Plenty  218.0935        NA #> 3 101.303 LERNZmp 1207285318 1219       TRUE Bay of Plenty 3373.5409  283.2226 #> 4  84.794 LERNZmp  524435311 1220       TRUE Bay of Plenty 1073.5467  311.2037 #> 5  94.291 LERNZmp 2490510485 1221       TRUE Bay of Plenty 4120.8868  300.7707 #> 6 125.195 LERNZmp  580255331 1222       TRUE Bay of Plenty  903.0982  347.0145 #>    id_final  gm_type name_final       dv   z_mean lernzmp_id #> 1 LID 40102 Volcanic     Rotoma 1.553606 46.15969   LID40102 #> 2 LID 40124      Dam   Matahina       NA 35.95600   LID40124 #> 3 LID 54730 Volcanic    Rotoiti 1.057013 35.69286   LID54730 #> 4 LID 54731 Volcanic   Okataina 1.720673 48.63426   LID54731 #> 5 LID 54732 Volcanic   Tarawera 1.919400 60.32738   LID54732 #> 6 LID 54733 Volcanic Rotomahana 1.532338 63.94700   LID54733 #>                         geometry #> 1 MULTIPOLYGON (((1915076 578... #> 2 MULTIPOLYGON (((1935526 576... #> 3 MULTIPOLYGON (((1896018 578... #> 4 MULTIPOLYGON (((1896494 577... #> 5 MULTIPOLYGON (((1895719 576... #> 6 MULTIPOLYGON (((1898882 575...  # Set membership lt_fetch("lake_metadata", filter = lt_filter(   id %in% c("LID40188", "LID40189") )) #> # A tibble: 2 × 7 #>   name    id       latitude longitude elevation depth    area #>   <chr>   <chr>       <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl> <dbl>   <int> #> 1 Rotoehu LID40188    -38.0      177.      303.  22.7 7965248 #> 2 Rotoehu LID40188    -38.0      177.      303.  22.7 7965248  # Null checks lt_fetch("water_profile", filter = lt_filter(   !is.na(value) )) #> # A tibble: 1,000 × 6 #>    site_id  datetime            parameter    value qc_level qc_comment #>    <chr>    <dttm>              <chr>        <dbl>    <int> <chr>      #>  1 Karapiro 2026-03-10 16:35:47 FlChlr       7.49       400 ""         #>  2 Karapiro 2026-03-10 16:35:47 FlChlr_AVG   7.49       400 ""         #>  3 Karapiro 2026-03-10 16:35:47 FlChlr_MED   6.79       400 ""         #>  4 Karapiro 2026-03-10 16:35:47 FlChlr_STDEV 1.43       400 ""         #>  5 Karapiro 2026-03-10 16:35:47 FlPhyc       2.72       400 ""         #>  6 Karapiro 2026-03-10 16:35:47 FlPhyc_AVG   2.72       400 ""         #>  7 Karapiro 2026-03-10 16:35:47 FlPhyc_MED   2.66       400 ""         #>  8 Karapiro 2026-03-10 16:35:47 FlPhyc_STDEV 0.158      400 ""         #>  9 Karapiro 2026-03-10 16:35:47 fDOM_AVG     9.63       400 ""         #> 10 Karapiro 2026-03-10 16:35:47 fDOM_MED     9.64       400 ""         #> # ℹ 990 more rows

title: ltapi - A package for accessing the LimnoTrack API
text: package caches API schema memory duration R session, repeated calls lt_tables(), lt_schema(), lt_fetch() re-download schema. force fresh fetch (e.g. database update):

what: Get (and cache) the OpenAPI spec — get_spec
title: Get (and cache) the OpenAPI spec — get_spec
text: Get (cache) OpenAPI spec

title: Get (and cache) the OpenAPI spec — get_spec

what: Reset the cached PostgREST schema — lt_cache_reset
title: Reset the cached PostgREST schema — lt_cache_reset
text: Clears cached API schema data, forcing next call lt_tables() lt_schema() re-fetch API.

title: Reset the cached PostgREST schema — lt_cache_reset
Reset the cached PostgREST schema — lt_cache_reset
text: Invisibly returns NULL.

title: Reset the cached PostgREST schema — lt_cache_reset

what: Query LimnoTrack database with flexible parameters — lt_fetch
title: Query LimnoTrack database with flexible parameters — lt_fetch
text: Query table LimnoTrack database optional parameters selecting columns, filtering rows, ordering results, pagination. Automatically detects geometry columns returns sf::sf object present, tibble::tibble otherwise.

title: Query LimnoTrack database with flexible parameters — lt_fetch
code: lt_fetch(   table,   select = NULL,   filter = NULL,   order = NULL,   limit = 1000L,   offset = 0L,   base_url = "https://api.limnotrack.com/postgrest" )

title: Query LimnoTrack database with flexible parameters — lt_fetch
text: table character; name table query (e.g., "lake_contours"). select character; comma-separated column names select (e.g., "col1,col2"). NULL, columns returned. filter named list PostgREST filter expressions, typically built lt_filter() (e.g., lt_filter(lernzmp_id == "LID40188")). order character; column ordering (e.g., "col1.asc,col2.desc"). limit integer; maximum number records return. Default 1000. offset integer; number records skip pagination. Default 0. base_url character; base URL LimnoTrack PostgREST API.

title: Query LimnoTrack database with flexible parameters — lt_fetch
text: tibble::tibble, sf::sf object table contains geometry data.

text: ltapi_api_unavailable API reached, returned 502/503/504. ltapi_api_error API returned unexpected HTTP error.
